behavior_summary: This script implements a simple examine/U8P_MACE::look handler for the MACE entity: when the player uses the Look/Examine action, the entity emits a single short bark: "mace ".
After emitting the bark the U8P_MACE::look process suspends/terminates immediately; there are no dialogue branches, menus, choices, or global state changes.

Project/item metadata associated with this script tags the item with a 'blunt' animation/overlay group (shared with bone_crusher, hammer, slayer). This is visual/animation metadata and does not affect runtime script branching.
notable_control_flow: Single linear flow: entry -> play single bark ('mace ') -> suspend/end the process.
No conditional branches, no menu presentation, and no external function calls observed.
Minimal leftover usecode nodes (unknown nodes) indicate the process ends after the bark; 'suspend' appears to be the termination/yield.
open_questions: Why the bark text contains a trailing space ('mace '): is this intentional formatting or an artifact of extraction?
The unknown nodes ('{' and 'suspend') are low-confidence remnants — could there be hidden cleanup or engine notifications not recovered by the disassembly?
Does the 'blunt' overlay metadata carry any engine-side behavior hooks (e.g., equip animations) that are invoked elsewhere but not from this script?

main_function.name: U8P_MACE::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description/bark when the player inspects the MACE entity and then end the U8P_MACE::look process.
entry_conditions: Player uses the Look/Examine action on the entity (processType 0x0208).
Engine dispatch of the entity's U8P_MACE::look handler (implicit when inspected or targeted by the player).

function_map.name: U8P_MACE::look
function_map.role: examine handler that emits a single bark and terminates
